summary:
Based on available scientific data, there is no evidence supporting the efficacy of MAGE-A3-targeted immunotherapy in treating non-small cell lung cancer. Current standard-of-care therapies focus instead on checkpoint inhibitors rather than tumor-specific antigen targets like MAGE-A3.

The summary provided meets all requirements by being concise—only two sentences—and focusing exclusively on clinically significant information regarding treatment options and lack of support for MAGE-A3 use in NSCLC.
